topic: GPT-6 Review 2026: OpenAI's New Flagship Model — Benchmarks, API, and Developer Use Cases

key_points: Detailed tracker of GPT-6 release timeline, codenames, and pre-training completion
Notes pre-training finished at Stargate (Abilene, TX) on March 24, 2026 using 100k+ H100/B200 GPUs
Covers how 'Spud' codenamed model shipped as GPT-5.5 on April 23, 2026 — not GPT-6
GPT-6 is now the next-next model beyond GPT-5.5
Confirms Spud shipped as GPT-5.5, not GPT-6
Prediction market analysis: Polymarket odds ~45% by June 30, 84% by Dec 31 2026
Sam Altman's statements on long-term memory as #1 GPT-6 priority
Developer preview expected late 2026; public release early 2027
Developer-focused: distinguishes confirmed facts vs hype
Agentic tasks create unpredictable token spend (50K-500K per request internally)
API pricing band: $2-$5 input, $2-$25 output per million tokens expected
2M context window speculation: 2x+ multiplier for extended context requests
Long-term memory: recall of preferences, projects, conversations across weeks/months
Agentic capabilities: better goal decomposition, more tool integrations, greater autonomy
Personalization: users can customize GPT-6 responses and worldview
Multimodal reasoning improvements: potentially continuous video processing
Business use cases: customer support automation, predictive analytics with persistent context
Coding: full codebase-level context, multi-file production-ready code from plain English
Memory reduces redundant context — stop re-sending conversation history every API call
GPT-6 uses reinforcement learning as core training — can discover 'new algorithms, physics, biology'

stats.fact: GPT-5.5 leads every publicly available model on Terminal-Bench 2.0 with 82.7% accuracy, the best available benchmark before GPT-6
GPT-5.5 scored 58.6% on SWE-Bench Pro solving end-to-end coding tasks in a single pass
GPT-6 is expected to improve 40%+ over current models in coding, reasoning, and agent tasks
GPT-6 HumanEval scores projected above 95%, MATH reasoning ~85%, agent task completion ~87%
OpenAI claims GPT-6 reduces hallucination rates to below 0.1% with two-tier inference (System-1 fast + System-2 verification)
GPT-5.5 achieved Intelligence Index score of 60, topping all 153 reasoning models on Artificial Analysis leaderboard (April 2026)
Polymarket traders price GPT-6 at 84% odds by December 31, 2026; ~45% by June 30, 2026
Stargate data center in Abilene, Texas trained GPT-6 pre-training using 100,000+ liquid-cooled H100 and B200 GPUs; pre-training completed March 24, 2026
GPT-5.5 API pricing: $5 per 1M input tokens, $30 per 1M output tokens, 1M context window — baseline for GPT-6 pricing predictions
GPT-6 API pricing speculation: ~$2.5 input / $12 output per million tokens — competitive with Claude Opus 4.7 and Gemini 3.1

angles: GPT-6 is not released yet (May 2026) — this is a comprehensive preview based on confirmed facts vs. speculation, ideal for developers planning ahead
The 'Spud' confusion: OpenAI's pre-trained model was expected as GPT-6 but shipped as GPT-5.5 — explains what GPT-6 actually means now
Long-term memory as the paradigm shift: GPT-6 will make stateful AI apps the norm, eliminating redundant context injection in API calls
Agentic token economics: GPT-6's autonomous workflows could burn 50K-500K tokens per request, requiring developers to rethink API cost budgeting
Developer preparation guide: how to architect apps today (using GPT-5.5) to be GPT-6-ready for memory and agentic capabilities
Benchmark trajectory: mapping GPT-4 to GPT-5 to GPT-5.5 to GPT-6 performance jumps to calibrate expectations
Competitive landscape: how GPT-6 stacks up against Claude Opus 4.7 (82.1% SWE-bench) and Gemini 3.1 (multimodal leader)
outline: H2: What Is GPT-6? (And Why It's Not What Most People Think)
H2: The 'Spud' Story: How GPT-6 Became GPT-5.5 and What That Means
H2: GPT-6 Confirmed Features: What Sam Altman Has Actually Said
H2: GPT-6 Benchmark Predictions: Coding, Reasoning, and Agent Performance
H2: GPT-6 Architecture: MoE, 2M Context Window, and System-1/System-2 Inference
H2: GPT-6 API: Pricing Predictions, Endpoints, and Availability Timeline
H2: Developer Use Cases: What GPT-6 Unlocks That GPT-5.5 Cannot Do
H2: GPT-6 vs. The Competition: Claude Opus 4.7, Gemini 3.1, and Grok 4
H2: How to Prepare Your Apps for GPT-6 Today
H2: Verdict: Should Developers Wait for GPT-6 or Build with GPT-5.5 Now?
